common_contraindications: Severe hepatic impairment; chronic alcohol use
notes: Most common OTC analgesic in India; hepatotoxic in overdose

common_contraindications: Penicillin allergy; infectious mononucleosis
notes: Broad-spectrum; causes rash in EBV infection

common_contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to PPIs
notes: Long-term use associated with magnesium depletion and fracture risk

common_contraindications: eGFR <30; metabolic acidosis; contrast dye procedures
notes: First-line type 2 diabetes; take with food

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; active liver disease; unexplained elevated transaminases
notes: Contraindicated in pregnancy due to teratogenicity

common_contraindications: Severe aortic stenosis; cardiogenic shock
notes: Long-acting; once daily dosing; ankle edema common

common_contraindications: Pregnancy (2nd/3rd trimester); angioedema history; bilateral renal artery stenosis
notes: Monitor potassium and creatinine; dry cough common side effect

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; bilateral renal artery stenosis
notes: Alternative to ACE inhibitors without cough side effect

common_contraindications: Severe bradycardia; heart block; cardiogenic shock; decompensated HF
notes: Do not stop abruptly; taper gradually

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; active bleeding; recent surgery; unsupervised patients
notes: Narrow therapeutic index; requires INR monitoring

common_contraindications: Active bleeding; severe hepatic impairment
notes: Prodrug requiring CYP2C19 activation; genetic polymorphism affects efficacy

common_contraindications: Active peptic ulcer; bleeding disorders; children with viral illness (Reye syndrome)
notes: Low dose (75-325mg) for cardiovascular prevention

common_contraindications: Active GI bleeding; severe renal impairment; third trimester pregnancy
notes: OTC analgesic; GI and cardiovascular risks with chronic use

common_contraindications: Active GI bleeding; severe hepatic/renal impairment; CABG surgery
notes: Available oral and topical; cardiovascular risk similar to COX-2 inhibitors

common_contraindications: Myasthenia gravis; tendon disorders; children <18 (generally)
notes: FDA black box for tendinitis and peripheral neuropathy

common_contraindications: Known macrolide hypersensitivity; hepatic impairment with prior azithromycin use
notes: QT prolongation risk; 3-5 day courses common

common_contraindications: Concurrent cisapride/pimozide; QT prolongation history
notes: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitor; many drug interactions

common_contraindications: Concurrent MAOIs (within 14 days); pimozide; thioridazine
notes: Long half-life (2-6 days active metabolite); FDA black box for suicidality in youth

common_contraindications: Concurrent MAOIs; pimozide
notes: Preferred SSRI in cardiac patients; fewer drug interactions than fluoxetine

common_contraindications: Known hypersensitivity
notes: Renal dose adjustment required; abuse potential recognized

common_contraindications: Known hypersensitivity
notes: Schedule V controlled substance; weight gain and edema common

common_contraindications: Concurrent MAOIs; seizure disorders; children <12
notes: Schedule IV; serotonergic properties increase interaction risk

common_contraindications: Concurrent delavirdine; pregnancy; sinus bradycardia; SA block
notes: Zero-order kinetics; small dose changes cause large level changes

common_contraindications: Concurrent MAOIs; bone marrow suppression; HLA-B*1502 positive
notes: Potent enzyme inducer; autoinduction of own metabolism

common_contraindications: Pregnancy (neural tube defects); hepatic disease; urea cycle disorders
notes: Broad spectrum anticonvulsant; teratogenic

common_contraindications: Uncorrected adrenal insufficiency; acute MI
notes: Take on empty stomach 30-60 min before food; many absorption interactions

common_contraindications: Systemic fungal infections; live vaccines during immunosuppressive doses
notes: Taper required after prolonged use; hyperglycemia common

common_contraindications: Hypoglycemia; known hypersensitivity
notes: Dose individualized; no maximum per se; monitor blood glucose

common_contraindications: Anuria; hepatic coma; severe electrolyte depletion
notes: Monitor electrolytes especially potassium; ototoxicity with rapid IV

common_contraindications: Hyperkalemia; Addison disease; anuria; concurrent eplerenone
notes: Anti-androgen effects; gynecomastia common in males

common_contraindications: Ventricular fibrillation; hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with obstruction
notes: Narrow therapeutic index (0.5-2.0 ng/mL); many drug interactions

common_contraindications: Cardiogenic shock; severe sinus node disease; second/third degree AV block
notes: Very long half-life (40-55 days); pulmonary and thyroid toxicity

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; active liver disease; concurrent strong CYP3A4 inhibitors
notes: 80mg dose restricted due to rhabdomyolysis risk

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; children <8 years (dental staining)
notes: Photosensitivity; take with adequate water; esophageal ulceration risk

common_contraindications: Pregnancy; nursing; alcoholism; chronic liver disease; immunodeficiency
notes: Folic acid supplementation required; weekly NOT daily dosing for RA

common_contraindications: Severe renal impairment; severe cardiovascular disease; dehydration
notes: Narrow therapeutic index (0.6-1.2 mEq/L); monitor levels closely

common_contraindications: Concurrent nitrates; severe hepatic impairment; recent stroke/MI
notes: PAH dose is 20mg TID; ED dose 25-100mg PRN

common_contraindications: Concurrent saquinavir/ritonavir; jaundice
notes: Most potent known CYP inducer; orange discoloration of body fluids

common_contraindications: Concurrent terfenadine/astemizole/cisapride; hepatic disease
notes: Oral form restricted due to hepatotoxicity; now mainly topical
source: OpenFDA ketoconazole label; FDA safety communication

common_contraindications: Acute liver disease; prior isoniazid hepatitis
notes: Pyridoxine (B6) supplementation recommended to prevent neuropathy

common_contraindications: Known hypersensitivity
notes: Slow titration required to reduce SJS/TEN risk; valproate doubles levels

common_contraindications: Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inhibitors in hepatic/renal impairment
notes: Narrow therapeutic index; GI toxicity dose-limiting
